_README: Bipolar contrastive prototypes for the Code Biology criteria embedding axis. Each criterion has its OWN theoretical opposite (a distinct axis); negatives are salted in surface form but kept ON that criterion's axis, NOT pooled into a generic 'mere chemistry' blob. adaptors<->arbitrariness are theoretically coupled (adaptor presence is the fingerprint of arbitrariness) so their negative axes legitimately correlate. instruct_* are the harrier query instructions ('Instruct: {task}\nQuery: {passage}'); paper text is embedded as a document with NO instruction. WIDENED 2026-06-14: 6 passages/pole, sharpened toward the extremes to open the within-criterion pos<->neg angle (see pole_separation['within']) so e-magnitudes carry signal, not just rank. Edit freely; the within-criterion pole-width diagnostic flags narrow/overlapping poles.

two_worlds.instruct_pos: Retrieve text arguing that the phenomenon involves two independent worlds of molecules with no necessary physical or chemical link between them
two_worlds.instruct_neg: Retrieve text arguing that the phenomenon is a single continuous chemical process in which the molecules are directly and necessarily linked
pos: The system involves two independent worlds of molecules: two distinct sets of objects with no necessary physical or chemical link between them, as codons and amino acids form separate molecular domains.

Two separate molecular populations exist whose correspondence is not fixed by their chemistry; the sequence of nucleotides and the sequence of amino acids belong to different, mutually independent worlds.
There are two distinct organic worlds, a world of signs and a world of meanings, that remain chemically independent of one another.
The two molecular sets are not reducible to each other; each could exist and vary independently of the chemistry of the other.

Crucially, there are two separate and irreducible molecular alphabets here, not one: the objects of the first set carry no chemical instruction for the objects of the second, and either set could be replaced without altering the other.
The defining fact is duality: a sign-world and a meaning-world coexist as two ontologically distinct molecular populations, and nothing in the makeup of one entails the makeup of the other.
neg: The process takes place within a single continuous chemical system; there are not two independent molecular worlds but one set of reactions determined by ordinary thermodynamics.
The two molecules are directly complementary by physical necessity, so their correspondence is fixed by chemistry and they do not constitute independent worlds.
The phenomenon is a straightforward physico-chemical interaction reducible to the properties of a single class of molecules.
One molecular species transforms directly into another along a chemically obligatory pathway, with no second, independent world involved.
There is no duality at all: a single molecular population undergoes a continuous reaction, and what looks like a second set is merely a further state of the same chemistry, fully entailed by it.
Everything reduces to one world of molecules whose behaviour is dictated end to end by mass action and binding energy; positing a separate, independent second world is unnecessary.

adaptors.instruct_pos: Retrieve text arguing that a third class of adaptor molecule physically bridges two independent molecular worlds
adaptors.instruct_neg: Retrieve text arguing that the two molecules interact directly, by catalysis or complementarity, with no mediating adaptor
pos: A third class of molecule, an adaptor such as transfer RNA, physically bridges the two worlds, recognising an object in one world and carrying the corresponding object from the other.
The mapping is implemented by dedicated adaptor molecules that connect the two independent sets; these adaptors are the molecular fingerprints of the code.
Bridging molecules stand between the two worlds and establish the correspondence, as aminoacyl-tRNAs link codons to amino acids.
The link between the two domains is realised by a separate set of mediator molecules rather than by direct contact between the domains themselves.

A distinct third party does the joining: an adaptor that touches one world at one end and the other world at the other end, so the two never meet directly and the adaptor alone fixes which goes with which.
The hallmark of the mechanism is a dedicated go-between molecule — neither sign nor meaning itself — whose two recognition surfaces are what physically instantiate the correspondence.
neg: The two molecules interact directly through complementary surfaces, with no third mediating adaptor required.
An enzyme catalyses the transformation of substrate into product; it speeds a chemical reaction rather than acting as an adaptor that maps one world onto another.
Recognition occurs by direct template complementarity between the two strands, without any intervening bridging molecule.
Binding is lock-and-key between the partners themselves, so no separate adaptor class mediates the interaction.
The partners contact each other face to face and that contact alone determines the outcome; there is no third go-between molecule anywhere in the mechanism.
Nothing mediates: the two molecules meet directly and their own complementary chemistry does all the work, leaving no role for any adaptor.

arbitrariness.instruct_pos: Retrieve text arguing that the coding rules are arbitrary conventions, compatible with but not dictated by physical law, and could in principle be otherwise
arbitrariness.instruct_neg: Retrieve text arguing that the correspondence is dictated by stereochemistry, affinity or thermodynamic necessity and could not be otherwise
pos: The coding rules are conventional rather than dictated by physical law: the correspondence is compatible with chemistry but not determined by it, and could in principle have been otherwise.
The assignment between the two worlds is arbitrary, a natural convention, as shown by the fact that the genetic code could be rewritten without violating any law of chemistry.
Nothing in the physics of the molecules forces this particular mapping; the rules are fixed by convention and alternative codes are equally possible.
The relationship is not determined by molecular affinity; it is a historically frozen convention that could vary.
The pairing is conventional to the core: swap the assignments and no law of physics or chemistry would be broken, which proves the mapping is chosen, not compelled.
Because countless alternative rule-sets are equally consistent with the underlying chemistry, the actual rules are an arbitrary historical accident, not a chemical inevitability.
neg: The specificity is dictated by stereochemistry: the pairing is fixed for steric reasons and could not be otherwise.
The correspondence follows necessarily from thermodynamics and molecular structure, so the rules are determined by chemistry rather than chosen.
Lock-and-key complementarity makes the interaction obligatory; the outcome is physically determined, not conventional.
The mapping is a direct consequence of chemical affinity and binding energy, leaving no freedom for alternative assignments.
There is exactly one possible outcome and physics fixes it: the geometry and energetics permit no alternative pairing, so the correspondence could not have been otherwise.
Far from arbitrary, the assignment is compelled by molecular shape and affinity — change nothing and the same pairing must result every time, because chemistry forbids the alternatives.

_controls.genetic_code_positive: In the genetic code, codons and amino acids are two independent molecular worlds bridged by transfer-RNA adaptors, and the assignment of codons to amino acids is an arbitrary convention that could be otherwise without breaking any law of chemistry.
_controls.deterministic_chemistry_negative: Enzyme specificity arises from stereochemical lock-and-key complementarity: the substrate binds the active site for steric and thermodynamic reasons, the reaction is a direct chemical transformation, and the outcome is physically determined rather than conventional.
